entities.name: Iron Triangle
entities.context: Triangulation method for human truth in hiring assessment, using three disparate planes to identify consistency

entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company; the assessment engine must be designed to detect narrative drift

entities.name: Careerspan assessment engine
entities.context: System being designed to look for narrative drift between assessment planes

entities.name: self-reflection
entities.context: First plane of the Iron Triangle; prone to blind spots bias

entities.name: interview performance
entities.context: Second plane of the Iron Triangle; prone to performance/charm bias

entities.name: external validation
entities.context: Third plane of the Iron Triangle; prone to networking bias

entities.name: narrative drift
entities.context: Inconsistency between the three assessment planes; indicates potential false narrative

entities.name: ground truth
entities.context: Reliable truth indicated by consistency across all three assessment planes

relationships.context: Self-reflection is one of the three planes in the triangulation method
Interview performance is one of the three planes in the triangulation method
External validation is one of the three planes in the triangulation method
Engine must be designed using Iron Triangle principles to detect narrative drift
Engine should flag candidates showing inconsistency across planes
Consistency across all three planes indicates ground truth
The assessment engine is a core component of Careerspan's hiring methodology
